framework.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
framework.disclaimer: NIST AI RMF is public domain. Shown for reference — verify against the official NIST publication.

controls.title: Govern
controls.statement: Govern function of the NIST AI Risk Management Framework.

children.title: Policies, Processes, Procedures, and Practices
children.statement: Policies and processes for AI risk management are in place, transparent, and implemented across the organization, including legal and regulatory understanding. (7 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Accountability Structures
children.statement: Accountability structures are in place so the right teams and individuals are empowered, responsible, and trained for AI risk management. (3 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Workforce Diversity and Inclusion
children.statement: Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility are prioritized in the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risk. (2 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Organizational Culture and Critical Thinking
children.statement: Organizational teams are committed to a culture that considers and communicates AI risk, encouraging critical thinking and safety-first mindsets. (3 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Stakeholder Engagement
children.statement: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors and affected communities, including mechanisms to collect and act on feedback. (2 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Third-Party Risk
children.statement: Policies address the risks and benefits of third-party AI software, data, and supply-chain components, including how they are acquired and monitored. (2 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

controls.title: Map
controls.statement: Map function of the NIST AI Risk Management Framework.

children.title: Context Establishment
children.statement: The context in which the AI system operates is established and understood, including intended purpose, settings, norms, and the organization's mission. (6 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Categorization
children.statement: The AI system, its capabilities, the tasks it performs, and the methods used are categorized and documented. (3 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: AI Capabilities, Targeted Usage, Goals
children.statement: The system's capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs are understood relative to appropriate benchmarks. (5 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Risk and Benefit Mapping of Third Parties
children.statement: Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system, including third-party software, data, and the broader value chain. (2 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Impacts on Individuals, Groups, and Society
children.statement: Impacts to individuals, groups, communities, organizations, and society are characterized, including the likelihood and magnitude of each identified impact. (2 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

controls.title: Measure
controls.statement: Measure function of the NIST AI Risk Management Framework.

children.title: Appropriate Methods and Metrics
children.statement: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied to measure the AI risks enumerated during the Map function. (3 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Trustworthiness Evaluation
children.statement: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics including validity, reliability, safety, security, resilience, privacy, fairness, bias, transparency, and explainability. (13 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Risk Tracking Mechanisms
children.statement: Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place, including for risks that are difficult to measure and emergent risks. (3 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Measurement Effectiveness
children.statement: Feedback about the efficacy of measurement is gathered and assessed, including input from domain experts and affected users. (3 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

controls.title: Manage
controls.statement: Manage function of the NIST AI Risk Management Framework.

children.title: Risk Prioritization and Response
children.statement: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output are prioritized, responded to, and managed, including decisions to proceed or not. (4 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Sustained Value and Risk Treatment
children.statement: Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and communicated. (4 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Third-Party Risk Management
children.statement: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed, including monitoring, contingency, and override mechanisms. (2 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)

children.title: Risk Monitoring and Communication
children.statement: Risk treatments, including response and recovery and post-deployment monitoring, are documented and regularly monitored, with appeal, override, and decommissioning mechanisms. (3 subcategories in NIST AI 100-1.)
